title: At the Wedding March
content:
God with honour hang your head,
    Groom, and grace you, bride, your bed
    With lissome scions, sweet scions,
    Out of hallowed bodies bred.

    Each be other's comfort kind:
    D�ep, d�eper than divined,
    Divine charity, dear charity,
    Fast you ever, fast bind.

    Then let the March tread our ears:
    I to him turn with tears
    Who to wedlock, his wonder wedlock,
    D�als tr�umph and immortal years.
meaning:
"At the Wedding March" is a quintessential example of Gerard Manley Hopkins's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
